title: Scout preproduction for day-part, weather, and phenology
summary: Seventeenth structured packet for Eco Explorer. The scout has finished the currently active depth backlog, so the best downtime use now is preproduction support for the approved living-world sequence. This packet created a small scout-only queue for a day-part mood matrix, a weather readability matrix, and a phenology readiness audit. All three planning-only handoffs are now complete, giving later implementation more grounded biome-by-biome mood, readability, and authored-content guidance.

execution_order.goal: Prepare a five-biome day-part mood matrix with readability guardrails.
execution_order.why_now: This is the cleanest preproduction input for `main-31` and helps future living-world work stay art-forward without becoming guesswork.

execution_order.goal: Prepare a biome weather readability matrix with atmospheric boundaries and emphasis rules.
execution_order.why_now: Weather is already approved, and this planning pass can prevent future visibility or clutter regressions before implementation begins.

execution_order.goal: Audit phenology-ready species, zones, and authored changes across the live five-biome chain.
execution_order.why_now: Phenology will be strongest if the content readiness work happens before implementation pressure makes it ad hoc.

guardrails: Science accuracy remains a hard gate.
These tasks are planning-only and should not start implementation work.
Keep the work mood-first and readability-first rather than simulation-heavy.
Tie recommendations to the live five-biome chain and the current `192x144` notebook/playfield constraints.
Prefer compact matrices and concrete authored examples over open-ended feature essays.
acceptance_focus: The scout stays productive without pulling implementation away from current work.
Later day-part, weather, and phenology work will have clearer biome-specific direction.
Future living-world work stays readable and authored instead of becoming a loose systems blob.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-living-world-sequence.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-living-world-grounding-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-phenology-grounding-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-living-world-preproduction-sequence.md, and this packet. Treat these scout tasks as planning-only preproduction for later living-world implementation, with a focus on biome-by-biome mood, readability, and authored content readiness.
